title: Farish Street Beauty Shop Sign
people: Farish Street shop owners and beauticians
description: Storefront sign from a Black-owned beauty shop that served the Farish Street business district.
significance: The sign represents Black entrepreneurship and the commercial life of Farish Street during segregation.
provenance: Donated by a former business owner's family.
studentSummary: This sign helps students see how Farish Street supported Black business owners, workers, and customers in Jackson.
classroomConnection: Use in lessons about segregation, local business history, and neighborhood economies.
notes: Surface paint loss along right edge.

title: Smith Robertson School Attendance Register
description: Attendance register documenting enrollment at Smith Robertson School.
significance: The register shows the importance of Black education in Jackson and documents daily school life.
provenance: Transferred from city school storage to museum archives.
studentSummary: This ledger shows that education was a major priority in Jackson's Black community.
classroomConnection: Use with discussions about access to education and community leadership.
notes: Digitized images available for classroom use.
